mechanism_explained: The IDK-IDK mechanism: engagement metrics decouple from learning when the chatbot becomes the relationship
Complexity threshold test: three conditions (adaptive sequencing / live instrumentation / multi-layered capability) determine when full architecture is warranted
Butter Knife Fallacy: judging the scalpel's potential by observing its average misuse; the Bastani GPT Base vs Tutor finding shows the guardrail design is the variable
Bolt-on failure: seven signals require integration the assembled-tools approach cannot produce; the layers are a loop not a stack
Decoupling problem: the artifact is no longer evidence of the process; seven GLP components reconstruct the friction signature that genuine learning leaves
Phase gates as measurement decisions: each mode is gated on a specific GLP signal so the mode arrives when the learner is ready for it
Within-learner contextual bandit: Thompson sampling over (mode × concept × time) cells with GLP-based reward; cross-learner generalization is outside the engine's scope by design
Backward design as the curriculum instrument: outcomes first, assessment second, content structure third — the concept map the engine needs cannot emerge from topic coverage
Cost-collapse asymmetry: AI content at marginal cost lowers burden of proof for low-risk content while leaving it unchanged for high-risk content
Binding-cost shift: when production cost collapses the binding constraint shifts to knowledge of the individual learner; cheap content makes PCK more valuable not less
Eight open questions as instrumented research bets: the platform is the experimental apparatus the questions are designed to answer
The map requires the thinking: Medhavy the instrument amplifies the design conversation the human conducts; the tool cannot replace it
